topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The attic air hung thick and dusty, smelling of aged paper and forgotten lavender. Liam shifted his weight on the rickety wooden trunk, his fingers tracing the worn leather binding. He hadn't meant to go up there, but a strange compulsion had pulled him toward the shadowy space. Beneath a pile of moth-eaten linens, he found it: a meticulously organized binder. Inside, every single report card, every essay, every crumpled worksheet from his entire academic career. A cold knot tightened in his stomach. He flipped through the pages, his breath catching in shallow gasps. It was overwhelming, this archive of his own efforts, and a tremor ran through his hands as he realized the sheer volume of completed work. He quickly snapped the binder shut, his heart hammering against his ribs, and started scrambling for the stairs.

[story 2]

Elara stood by the kitchen counter, staring at the stack of textbooks her mother had left on the dining table. It wasn't just books; it was a collection of meticulously labeled folders, each containing assignments from middle school through college. A strange, unsettling feeling settled over her, making the silence of the apartment feel too loud. She picked up a sheet of paper—a history project from years ago—and her fingers felt clumsy, trembling slightly as she unfolded it. "Mom must have kept all of this," she whispered to the empty room. The weight of the responsibility, the sheer scope of what her mother had preserved, pressed down on her chest. She backed away slowly, her eyes darting toward the hallway, needing an escape from the intense pressure building inside her.

[story 3]

Mark found the hidden box tucked behind a loose panel in the basement storage room. It was a cardboard container, surprisingly heavy, filled to the brim with schoolwork. As he lifted the lid, the scent of old pencil shavings filled his nostrils. His palms began to sweat, and he gripped the edges of the box so tightly his knuckles turned white. Every document seemed to hold a weight, a silent judgment. He pulled out a science lab report, his gaze flickering rapidly across the dense paragraphs. He swallowed hard, the dryness in his throat making the action difficult. He felt utterly exposed, as if he were being scrutinized by every teacher who had ever graded his work. He closed the box with a soft, definitive thud, needing to leave the space immediately.
